perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will endeavor to keep the Confidential Information in confidence and may disclose Confidential Information if deemed necessary. However, the Receiving Party is encouraged to seek the Company's consent before disclosure, whenever feasible. Disclosure to Representatives is allowed as long as they acknowledge receipt.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'shall' with 'endeavor', making confidentiality an aspiration rather than a requirement, and replacing explicit conditions for disclosure with a vague 'if deemed necessary' standard, this introduces ambiguity. It is creating an in-text contradiction, particularly with Section 11, which outlines remedies for breaches of the agreement, including an injunction, assuming strict confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made 5 whole by monetary damages only. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction (which shall include a temporary restraining order) to prevent breaches of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates strict confidentiality ('shall not disclose'). The changed text weakens this to an aspirational 'will endeavor' and allows disclosure 'if deemed necessary,' contradicting the strict confidentiality implied in the contradicted text. Section 11 entitles the parties to injunctive relief to prevent breaches, implying that any breach is a serious violation of a strict confidentiality obligation. The changed text introduces ambiguity and discretion, making it unclear when disclosure is permissible, directly conflicting with the assumption of strict confidentiality required for injunctive relief and remedies for breaches.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon reasonable request from the Company, the Receiving Party will make efforts to return available copies of Confidential Information, but is not obligated to destroy any notes, studies, or reports derived from it.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies' to 'Upon reasonable request from the Company, the Receiving Party will make efforts to return available copies'. Also adding the text 'but is not obligated to destroy any notes, studies, or reports derived from it'. This introduces ambiguity concerning the completeness of the return, and the destruction of the notes, studies, and reports and contradicts with Section 7 itself which states 'Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof.', thus creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information . Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term 4 hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both state that 'all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed' will be returned. However, the changed text states that the Receiving Party will make efforts to return available copies of Confidential Information, but is not obligated to destroy any notes, studies, or reports derived from it. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, as it no longer requires the return of all copies or the destruction of related documents, thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom it discloses the Confidential Information receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information under the terms of a subpoena or other order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or by another governmental agency, the Receiving Party shall if permitted pursuant to applicable law (a) promptly notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (b) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking steps to resist or narrow such request, (c) if disclosure of such Confidential Information or Discussion Information is required, furnish only such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information as the Receiving Party is advised by its legal counsel is legally required to be disclosed and (d) if requested by the Company, cooperate with the Company in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other relief to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to such portion of the Confidential Information or Discussion Information, as applicable, that is required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Receiving Party receives a request to disclose Confidential Information under a subpoena, it will consider notifying the Company, but is ultimately responsible for determining the appropriate response under applicable law. Cooperation with the Company’s efforts to obtain a protective order is at the Receiving Party’s discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall if permitted pursuant to applicable law (a) promptly notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request' to 'will consider notifying the Company' and 'cooperate with the Company in its efforts to obtain a protective order or other relief' to 'Cooperation with the Company’s efforts to obtain a protective order is at the Receiving Party’s discretion', makes the Receiving Party responsibilities more flexible and contradicting with the original responsibilities for confidentiality of Section 3(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligated the Receiving Party to 'promptly notify' and 'cooperate' in the event of a subpoena. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'will consider' and makes cooperation 'at the Receiving Party’s discretion.' This weakens the obligation to protect Confidential Information. The contradicted text states 'The Receiving Party shall... keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law... provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof'. Section 9 previously stipulated a mandatory notification and cooperation process, which is now discretionary, thus creating a direct conflict with the 'shall not disclose' obligation and the requirement to comply with Section 9 before disclosure. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges that it is aware and that the Receiving Party and its Representatives have been advised that the United States securities laws prohibit any person having non-public material information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of that company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party acknowledges it has been informed that securities laws may restrict trading on non-public information and will generally advise its Representatives to be mindful of such laws. However, the Receiving Party does not assume responsibility for their individual compliance.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'The Receiving Party acknowledges that it is aware and that the Receiving Party and its Representatives have been advised that the United States securities laws prohibit any person having non-public material information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of that company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.' to 'The Receiving Party acknowledges it has been informed that securities laws may restrict trading on non-public information and will generally advise its Representatives to be mindful of such laws. However, the Receiving Party does not assume responsibility for their individual compliance.', weakens the Receiving Party's acknowledgment of and responsibility related to securities laws, thus creating an in-text contradiction, specially if in section 3(a) we have that 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, keep the Confidential Information in confidence and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information if required by applicable law, regulation or legal or regulatory process; provided, that the Receiving Party shall have first complied with the terms of Section 9 hereof, (ii) the Receiving Party may make disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information to the extent that the Company gives its prior written consent, and (iii) any information contained in the Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who agree to keep such information in confidence to the same extent as described herein; provided , further , that the Receiving Party shall not make any disclosure of any Confidential Information to any potential sources of equity financing without the Company’s prior written consent. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text emphasizes the Receiving Party's awareness and responsibility regarding securities laws. The changed text weakens this, stating the Receiving Party will 'generally advise' its representatives but does not assume responsibility for their compliance. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or by any of its Representatives.' The change introduces ambiguity about the Receiving Party's liability for its representatives' actions regarding confidential information and securities laws, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because the responsibility regarding the representatives is now inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement outlines the general understanding between the parties, and amendments may be agreed upon through mutual consent, documented through email or other informal communication. Such amendments will be considered valid if both parties act in accordance with them.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.' to 'and amendments may be agreed upon through mutual consent, documented through email or other informal communication. Such amendments will be considered valid if both parties act in accordance with them.', this undermines the clause that this agreement represents the entire understanding and that it can be modified only through a signed document. Modifying this can create a contradiction in case the Company needs to bring legal actions given the informality of the modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement; Amendments . This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted text. The original text explicitly states that amendments must be in writing and executed by both parties, while the changed text allows for amendments through informal communication such as email, creating a clear contradiction regarding the validity and enforceability of modifications to the agreement. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
